name: djangorestframework-simplejwt

metadata.name: djangorestframework-simplejwt
metadata.summary: A minimal JSON Web Token authentication plugin for Django REST Framework

## Abstract

Simple JWT is a JSON Web Token authentication plugin for the [Django REST
Framework](http://www.django-rest-framework.org/).

For full documentation, visit [django-rest-framework-simplejwt.readthedocs.io](https://django-rest-framework-simplejwt.readthedocs.io/en/latest/).

## Translations

Contribute translations directly with PRs or via inlang <https://inlang.com/editor/github.com/jazzband/djangorestframework-simplejwt>

functions.name: rest_framework_simplejwt.utils.get_md5_hash_password
documentation: Returns MD5 hash of the given password

methods.name: rest_framework_simplejwt.exceptions.DetailDictMixin.__init__
documentation:
Builds a detail dictionary for the error to give more information to API
users.

methods.name: rest_framework_simplejwt.backends.TokenBackend._validate_algorithm
documentation:
Ensure that the nominated algorithm is recognized, and that cryptography is installed for those
algorithms that require it

methods.name: rest_framework_simplejwt.backends.TokenBackend.encode
documentation: Returns an encoded token for the given payload dictionary.

methods.name: rest_framework_simplejwt.backends.TokenBackend.decode
documentation:
Performs a validation of the given token and returns its payload
dictionary.

Raises a `TokenBackendError` if the token is malformed, if its
signature check fails, or if its 'exp' claim indicates it has expired.

methods.name: rest_framework_simplejwt.views.TokenViewBase.get_serializer_class
documentation: If serializer_class is set, use it directly. Otherwise get the class from settings.

classes.name: rest_framework_simplejwt.views.TokenObtainPairView
documentation:
Takes a set of user credentials and returns an access and refresh JSON web
token pair to prove the authentication of those credentials.

classes.name: rest_framework_simplejwt.views.TokenRefreshView
documentation:
Takes a refresh type JSON web token and returns an access type JSON web
token if the refresh token is valid.

classes.name: rest_framework_simplejwt.views.TokenObtainSlidingView
documentation:
Takes a set of user credentials and returns a sliding JSON web token to
prove the authentication of those credentials.

classes.name: rest_framework_simplejwt.views.TokenRefreshSlidingView
documentation:
Takes a sliding JSON web token and returns a new, refreshed version if the
token's refresh period has not expired.

classes.name: rest_framework_simplejwt.views.TokenVerifyView
documentation:
Takes a token and indicates if it is valid.  This view provides no
information about a token's fitness for a particular use.

classes.name: rest_framework_simplejwt.views.TokenBlacklistView
documentation:
Takes a token and blacklists it. Must be used with the
`rest_framework_simplejwt.token_blacklist` app installed.

classes.name: rest_framework_simplejwt.models.TokenUser

methods.name: rest_framework_simplejwt.models.TokenUser.__getattr__
documentation: This acts as a backup attribute getter for custom claims defined in Token serializers.

A dummy user class modeled after django.contrib.auth.models.AnonymousUser.
Used in conjunction with the `JWTStatelessUserAuthentication` backend to
implement single sign-on functionality across services which share the same
secret key.  `JWTStatelessUserAuthentication` will return an instance of this
class instead of a `User` model instance.  Instances of this class act as
stateless user objects which are backed by validated tokens.

classes.name: rest_framework_simplejwt.authentication.JWTAuthentication

methods.name: rest_framework_simplejwt.authentication.JWTAuthentication.get_header
documentation:
Extracts the header containing the JSON web token from the given
request.

methods.name: rest_framework_simplejwt.authentication.JWTAuthentication.get_raw_token
documentation:
Extracts an unvalidated JSON web token from the given "Authorization"
header value.

methods.name: rest_framework_simplejwt.authentication.JWTAuthentication.get_validated_token
documentation:
Validates an encoded JSON web token and returns a validated token
wrapper object.

methods.name: rest_framework_simplejwt.authentication.JWTAuthentication.get_user
documentation: Attempts to find and return a user using the given validated token.

An authentication plugin that authenticates requests through a JSON web
token provided in a request header.

classes.name: rest_framework_simplejwt.authentication.JWTStatelessUserAuthentication

methods.name: rest_framework_simplejwt.authentication.JWTStatelessUserAuthentication.get_user
documentation:
Returns a stateless user object which is backed by the given validated
token.

An authentication plugin that authenticates requests through a JSON web
token provided in a request header without performing a database lookup to obtain a user instance.

classes.name: rest_framework_simplejwt.tokens.Token

methods.name: rest_framework_simplejwt.tokens.Token.__init__
documentation:
!!!! IMPORTANT !!!! MUST raise a TokenError with a user-facing error
message if the given token is invalid, expired, or otherwise not safe
to use.

methods.name: rest_framework_simplejwt.tokens.Token.__str__
documentation: Signs and returns a token as a base64 encoded string.

methods.name: rest_framework_simplejwt.tokens.Token.verify
documentation:
Performs additional validation steps which were not performed when this
token was decoded.  This method is part of the "public" API to indicate
the intention that it may be overridden in subclasses.

methods.name: rest_framework_simplejwt.tokens.Token.verify_token_type
documentation: Ensures that the token type claim is present and has the correct value.

methods.name: rest_framework_simplejwt.tokens.Token.set_jti
documentation:
Populates the configured jti claim of a token with a string where there
is a negligible probability that the same string will be chosen at a
later time.

See here:
https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7519#section-4.1.7

methods.name: rest_framework_simplejwt.tokens.Token.set_exp
documentation:
Updates the expiration time of a token.

See here:
https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7519#section-4.1.4

methods.name: rest_framework_simplejwt.tokens.Token.set_iat
documentation:
Updates the time at which the token was issued.

See here:
https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7519#section-4.1.6

methods.name: rest_framework_simplejwt.tokens.Token.check_exp
documentation:
Checks whether a timestamp value in the given claim has passed (since
the given datetime value in `current_time`).  Raises a TokenError with
a user-facing error message if so.

methods.name: rest_framework_simplejwt.tokens.Token.for_user
documentation:
Returns an authorization token for the given user that will be provided
after authenticating the user's credentials.

A class which validates and wraps an existing JWT or can be used to build a
new JWT.

classes.name: rest_framework_simplejwt.tokens.BlacklistMixin

methods.name: rest_framework_simplejwt.tokens.BlacklistMixin.check_blacklist
documentation:
Checks if this token is present in the token blacklist.  Raises
`TokenError` if so.

methods.name: rest_framework_simplejwt.tokens.BlacklistMixin.blacklist
documentation:
Ensures this token is included in the outstanding token list and
adds it to the blacklist.

methods.name: rest_framework_simplejwt.tokens.BlacklistMixin.for_user
documentation: Adds this token to the outstanding token list.

If the `rest_framework_simplejwt.token_blacklist` app was configured to be
used, tokens created from `BlacklistMixin` subclasses will insert
themselves into an outstanding token list and also check for their
membership in a token blacklist.

methods.name: rest_framework_simplejwt.tokens.RefreshToken.access_token
documentation:
Returns an access token created from this refresh token.  Copies all
claims present in this refresh token to the new access token except
those claims listed in the `no_copy_claims` attribute.

methods.name: rest_framework_simplejwt.tokens.UntypedToken.verify_token_type
documentation:
Untyped tokens do not verify the "token_type" claim.  This is useful
when performing general validation of a token's signature and other
properties which do not relate to the token's intended use.
